name: l4-vscode
description: Write, test, and deploy legal rules as executable code — with a built-in Legalese AI chat, MCP for AI agents, WebMCP for browser AI, syntax highlighting, IntelliSense, ladder diagrams, and cloud deployment

jl4.serverExecutablePath.markdownDescription: Path to the `jl4-lsp` executable. Leave empty to use the bundled binary or find `jl4-lsp` on your PATH.

jl4.trace.server.description: Traces the communication between VS Code and the language server.

jl4.serviceUrl.markdownDescription: URL of the jl4-service for deploying rules (e.g. `http://localhost:8080`). Set this for self-hosted deployments. Leave empty to use Legalese Cloud login.

jl4.serviceApiKey.markdownDescription: API key (`sk_...`) for authenticating with a self-hosted jl4-service. Not needed when using Legalese Cloud login.

jl4.mcpPort.markdownDescription: Local port for the L4 MCP proxy server. Legalese AI (built in), Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and other MCP-speaking agents connect to this port to access L4 rules.

legaleseAi.apiKey.markdownDescription: API key for authenticating with Legalese AI. When set, this key is used instead of your Legalese Cloud session.

legaleseAi.localMode.markdownDescription: Run Legalese AI in local mode for development purposes.

legaleseAi.showReasoning.description: Show the model's reasoning ("Thinking" blocks) in the Legalese AI chat.

legaleseAi.methodology.description: Guidance the Legalese AI follows when encoding natural language rules in L4.

legaleseAi.mcp.disabledServers.description: MCP servers switched off for Legalese AI (by mcp.json key).

legaleseAi.mcp.disabledTools.description: Individual MCP tools switched off for Legalese AI, as server::tool.

legaleseAi.permissions.readFiles.description: Legalese AI permission: read files and folders in the workspace.

legaleseAi.permissions.createFiles.description: Legalese AI permission: create new files in the workspace.

legaleseAi.permissions.editFiles.description: Legalese AI permission: edit files in the workspace.

legaleseAi.permissions.deleteFiles.description: Legalese AI permission: delete (trash) files in the workspace.

legaleseAi.permissions.evaluateL4.description: Legalese AI permission: run L4 diagnostics and directives via the LSP.

legaleseAi.permissions.refactorL4.description: Legalese AI permission: apply structured L4 refactors.

legaleseAi.permissions.runDeployedRules.description: Legalese AI permission: browse and evaluate deployed L4 rules on Legalese Cloud.

legaleseAi.permissions.vscodeMcp.description: Legalese AI permission: call tools from the user's MCP servers. Which servers/tools are reachable at all is controlled by the toggles in the sidebar's MCP servers section.

legaleseAi.mcp.enabled.description: Master switch: expose the user's MCP servers to Legalese AI at all.

legaleseAi.permissions.askUser.description: Legalese AI permission: pause the chat to ask you clarifying questions.

legaleseAi.permissions.statusUpdate.description: Legalese AI permission: post short status updates into the chat.

chatParticipants.name: legalese
chatParticipants.description: Computational law: encode, deploy, and reason about L4 rules.

languageModelTools.name: l4_evaluate
languageModelTools.userDescription: Type-check an L4 file and report the results of its #EVAL / #CHECK / #ASSERT / #TRACE directives.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Evaluate an L4 (rules-as-code) file via the jl4 language server. Returns the latest results of every directive in the file (#EVAL, #EVALTRACE, #CHECK, #TRACE, #ASSERT). If the file has type errors, returns the compiler diagnostics instead — fix those first, then call again. Use this after creating or editing any .l4 file to verify the rules behave as intended.

path.description: Path to the .l4 file, relative to the workspace root (absolute paths inside the workspace also work).

mode.description: "full" (default) reports every directive; "changed" reports only directives added or whose value changed since the last call.

timeoutMs.description: How long to wait (ms) for the language server to finish compiling if no results are cached yet. Default 6000, max 15000.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run lint && npm run bundle-esbuild
scripts.build: npm run clean && npm run prepare:webview && tsc -b tsconfig.json && npm run bundle-esbuild
scripts.package: npm run lint && npx vsce package
scripts.clean: git clean -fdx out || echo 'git not available'
